_comment: Bit-flag registry for the `cards.flags` (u32), `actions.flags`, and `magnetic_actions.flags` columns. Each entry pins an explicit bit position: single-bit flags use `bit` (a number), multi-bit fields use `bits` (an array of bit positions, low-to-high). Append-only — once a bit (or set of bits) is assigned a name, never reuse it for a different meaning. Removed flags / fields should leave a `_reserved_<bit>` tombstone so a future feature can't silently inherit the old semantics.

cards._comment: Bits in `Card.flags` (u8). Freshly-inserted cards have all bits clear. Server-side: `cards::insert_card_row` zero-initializes the field; specific bits are set/cleared by the feature that introduced them.

position_hold.description: **Temporary** position hold — clears once the holder is done. Set by an active user drag, an in-flight animation, or the server while a magnetic action is using this card as a slot filler; cleared by the same system on drop / animation end / action end. While set: the user cannot pick the card up, and automatic layout must not reposition it.

position_locked.description: **Permanent** position lock — never clears under normal play. Set on cards whose location is part of their identity (world tiles in their cells, anchored event cards, etc.). While set: the user cannot pick the card up, ever; only deliberate admin / migration code clears it.

surface_locked.description: Card is locked to its current surface and may not be moved to a different surface.

drop_hold.description: **Temporary** drop hold — card is currently mid-drop and must not be re-picked or repositioned until the drop resolves.

drop_locked.description: **Permanent** drop lock — card cannot be dropped at all; attempts to drop it must be rejected.

slot_hold.description: **Temporary** slot reservation — set by the server when a recipe match has claimed this card as a slot filler and execution is pending. While set: the card is committed to the in-flight recipe and must not be re-claimed by another match, picked up by the user, or moved by automatic layout. Cleared when the recipe completes, fails, or is cancelled.

_reserved_6.description: Tombstone — formerly `display_progress` (single-bit on/off marker). Replaced by `progress_style` (3-bit field at bits 8..=10) once style variants were needed. Don't reuse this bit for an unrelated meaning.

dead.description: Server has marked this card as dead — typically a recipe consumed it as a reagent. Set via UPDATE (rather than DELETE) so the write carries `valid_at` (the server's unix-seconds 'this happened at' timestamp); the client treats the bit flip as the death event and times its dying animation against `valid_at` instead of receive time. Cards-only — actions and magnetic_actions delete immediately and have no equivalent bit.

progress_style.description: 3-bit field encoding the progress bar style for this row's 'next future event' client render. The client reads `(flags >> 8) & 0b111` and renders accordingly. Values: 0 = no progress (no bar shown), 1 = ltr / cw default, 2 = rtl / ccw default. Values 3..=7 are reserved for future styles. Set on the actor card's completion row by `action_completion`; cleared on with-holds rows so non-event rows don't render bars. Multiple queued events on one card stack as a sequence of future rows each carrying their own style.

force_position.description: Server is asserting this row's `micro_zone` / `micro_location` verbatim — the client mirror should overwrite whatever it had locally and renumber any conflicting entries. Set on with-holds rows where the server has just stitched a chain layout (`propose_action`); the client treats this as 'discard your local position guess, this is the truth'. Cleared on rows where the position is just a natural state transition (release, completion, free creation) — those are propagated by the normal valid_at promotion path without needing the override. Replaces the older 'set `micro_zone.local_q = 1` as a disagree-with-anything trigger' hack — that bit had to share a field with real q-coordinate data, leaving ambiguity; a dedicated flag bit keeps the signaling explicit.

magnetic.description: **Temporary** — server has installed a magnetic action on this card. While set: the card is acting as the anchor of an in-flight magnetic recipe (the `magnetic_actions` row keyed on it is ticking every `recipe.interval` seconds, pulling inventory cards onto the anchor's chain). Set by the magnetic-install path of `on_create::trigger` when a recipe's `magnetic` bucket fires; cleared by the magnetic tick reducer when the action terminates — either by successfully filling an inner recipe's slot list (queueing the inner action and firing the outer's `output_success`) or by exhausting the loop-count cap (firing `output_failure`). Held semantics: cleared on completion alongside the other `*_hold` bits via `release_mask`.
